name: Lima Bean & Fennel Stew
recipeInstructions:
1. Pick over and wash the lima beans, then soak them in water to cover for at least 6 hours or overnight. Drain the soaking water.
2. Place the limas in a large stewpot with fresh water. Bring to a boil, then lower the heat and simmer gently for 1 hour.
3. Scrub the potatoes and carrots. Cut the potatoes into 1/2-inch dice and the carrots into half lengthwise, then crosswise into 1/4-inch slices. Add the vegetables to the limas.
4. In a separate pan, sauté the onion in 2 tablespoons olive oil until golden brown.
5. Crush the fennel seed lightly in a mortar. Add it along with thyme, marjoram, garlic, and 2 tablespoons water to the onion. Cook over low heat for 3 minutes to blend flavors.
6. Add the wine and simmer until the liquid is reduced slightly. Scrape the onion mixture into the bean mixture and return the pan to the burner.
7. Sauté the mushrooms in the remaining tablespoon oil until golden around the edges. Add the mushrooms to the stew, adding more water if necessary to prevent sticking.
8. Add the diced fennel bulb, bay leaf, tamari, and lemon. Simmer about 10 minutes or until the fennel bulb is just tender. Serve immediately.
